acceptance_criteria.description: Native replay formats are preserved inside atomically written, content-sealed worldeval/replay-bundle/1.0.0 directories that reject unsafe or inconsistent artifacts.
A deterministic demo saves an authority replay before completion and independently verifies it offline with zero provider calls.
Environment-neutral replay routes expose only allow-listed public files while legacy replay APIs remain compatible.

compatibility: Wrap existing native replay formats without changing their bytes or existing route contracts.
migration: Normalize future demos first; migrate legacy bundles through additive outer manifests.
privacy: Public bundles exclude credentials, prompts, provider output, protected observations, and private authority state.
rollback: A failed media render or bundle promotion cannot delete or invalidate a sealed native replay.

in: Public replay discovery and safe file access
out: Treating authored presentation scripts as authority evidence

summary: Standardize replay-first demo evidence while retaining every environment's authoritative native replay format.
title: Universal replay-bundle standard
